description:
You are helping a developer establish *project-local* time-zone and locale settings so that team members do **not** have to touch any system-wide configuration files.  
Carry out the following steps from a normal, non-root shell.  
When you are finished, every path, file, and piece of content described below **must exist exactly as written** so that our automated grader can find and verify them.

1. Directory lay-out  
   a. Create the directory “/home/user/dev_project”.  
   b. Inside it create three sub-directories exactly named  
      • “config”  
      • “scripts”  
      • “logs”

   The three directories must therefore be present at the following absolute paths:  
      /home/user/dev_project/config  
      /home/user/dev_project/scripts  
      /home/user/dev_project/logs

2. Configuration files (two small one-liners) – place them in /home/user/dev_project/config  

   • /home/user/dev_project/config/timezone.conf  
     └── Its single line of text must be  
         TZ=Asia/Tokyo  

   • /home/user/dev_project/config/locale.conf  
     └── Its single line of text must be  
         LANG=ja_JP.UTF-8  

   Both files must end with a single trailing newline character (Unix “\n”).

3. Helper script – place it in /home/user/dev_project/scripts  

   • Filename: /home/user/dev_project/scripts/apply_env.sh  
   • The script must be written in POSIX-compatible Bash, must be marked executable, and must do *exactly* the following when run (or when sourced):  
        1) Read the two *.conf* files created in step 2 (you may `source` them or parse them – either is fine).  
        2) Export the variables TZ and LANG that it finds in those files to the current shell environment.  
        3) Immediately write to STDOUT the single line:  
              Environment applied: $(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z') ($LANG)  
           The timestamp and zone produced by `date` should of course reflect the newly applied TZ.

4. Verification log – must be produced at /home/user/dev_project/logs/env_validation.log  
   After using the helper script to change the environment, create the text file “env_validation.log” with **exactly** the four lines shown below, in exactly this order, each terminated by a newline (Unix “\n”):

       NEW_TZ=Asia/Tokyo
       NEW_LANG=ja_JP.UTF-8
       DATE_IN_NEW_ENV=1970-01-01 09:00:00 JST
       STATUS=success

   • To obtain the third line you will need to run  
       date -d '@0' +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z'  
     *after* the environment variables have been exported by the helper script.  
   • Do **not** include any extra spaces or blank lines.  
   • Make sure the log file ends with a newline after the final “STATUS=success” line.

Important rules the grader will enforce  
– Every file must be located at the exact absolute paths listed above.  
– File names are case-sensitive.  
– The contents of each file must match **exactly** (including capitalisation, punctuation and final newlines).  
– The script apply_env.sh must have the executable bit set (chmod +x).  
– Only the four log lines listed in step 4 are allowed in env_validation.log.

If everything is correct the automated tests will find:

  • the three required directories,  
  • the two one-line *.conf* files with the precise strings,  
  • an executable apply_env.sh whose behaviour sets TZ/LANG for the current shell, and  
  • an env_validation.log whose four lines exactly match the specification above.

No system-level changes (e.g. /etc/timezone) are necessary—keep everything inside /home/user/dev_project.  
Good luck!
truth:
Required final state for automated checks:

1. Directories:
   /home/user/dev_project
   /home/user/dev_project/config
   /home/user/dev_project/scripts
   /home/user/dev_project/logs

2. Files and exact contents (each ends with a trailing \n):

/home/user/dev_project/config/timezone.conf
------------------------------------------------
TZ=Asia/Tokyo\n

/home/user/dev_project/config/locale.conf
------------------------------------------------
LANG=ja_JP.UTF-8\n

/home/user/dev_project/scripts/apply_env.sh
------------------------------------------------
(Grader will only check that the file exists, is executable, and that running
   `bash -c "source /home/user/dev_project/scripts/apply_env.sh && date -d '@0' +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z'"` 
   prints "1970-01-01 09:00:00 JST")
   Exact implementation text is **not** verified.

/home/user/dev_project/logs/env_validation.log
------------------------------------------------
NEW_TZ=Asia/Tokyo\n
NEW_LANG=ja_JP.UTF-8\n
DATE_IN_NEW_ENV=1970-01-01 09:00:00 JST\n
STATUS=success\n

Permissions:
• apply_env.sh must have mode 755 (or at minimum executable by user).
Other files can be 644.

The grader will fail the task if any of the following is true:
– Any required directory is missing.
– Any required file is missing.
– Any file’s contents differ, even by one character or missing newline.
– env_validation.log has more or fewer than 4 lines or the lines are out of order.
– apply_env.sh is not executable or, when sourced, does not set TZ to Asia/Tokyo and LANG to ja_JP.UTF-8.
